title: Coal Mines Regulations, 2017 — Consolidated Amendments (2018–2024)
summary: A single consolidated gazette compilation, published by DGMS on 3 July 2024, bringing together the amendments made to the Coal Mines Regulations, 2017 between 2018 and 2024. DGMS issues these changes as one combined document rather than separate year-by-year notifications, so this file is the reference point for the current amended text of CMR 2017.
status_note: Amends CMR 2017; CMR 2017 remains operative.

title: CMR 2017 Amendment — Scale of Appointment of Assistant Manager (Reg. 30)
summary: Amendment to the Coal Mines Regulations, 2017 notified as G.S.R. 640(E) on 9 October 2025, revising the scale for appointment of Assistant Managers in coal mines (the Regulation 30 cluster). It updates the manpower-to-management ratios coal mine operators must maintain for statutory supervisory staffing.
status_note: Amends CMR 2017 (Reg. 30); CMR 2017 remains operative.

title: CMR 2017 Amendment — Gas Testing Certificate (2025)
summary: A 2025 amendment to the Coal Mines Regulations, 2017 dealing with the Gas Testing Certificate, part of the competency and statutory-testing framework for persons authorised to test for inflammable gas underground.
status_note: Amends CMR 2017; CMR 2017 remains operative. (Related 2021 competency bye-laws withdrawn 23 Mar 2026.)

title: Draft OSH&WC (Coal Mines) Regulations, 2026
summary: The Draft Occupational Safety, Health and Working Conditions (Coal Mines) Regulations, 2026, published as G.S.R. 67(E) on 28 January 2026 for a 45-day public consultation. Framed under Section 136 of the OSH&WC Code, 2020 (not the Mines Act, 1952), these draft regulations are intended eventually to replace the Coal Mines Regulations, 2017. IMPORTANT: this is a DRAFT and is NOT in force — CMR 2017 remains the operative law.
status_note: Draft — 45-day public consultation; NOT in force. CMR 2017 remains operative.

title: Mines Act, 1952
summary: The Mines Act, 1952 is the parent legislation for occupational safety, health and welfare in all mines in India. It establishes the Directorate General of Mines Safety (DGMS), defines the duties of owners, agents and managers, regulates working hours, leave and employment of persons, and empowers the Central Government to frame regulations, rules and bye-laws such as the Coal Mines Regulations. It is the statutory foundation on which mine-specific safety law rests.
status_note: Subsumed by the OSH&WC Code, 2020 (in force 21 Nov 2025); subordinate rules continue under savings.

title: Mines Rules, 1955
summary: The Mines Rules, 1955 are framed under the Mines Act, 1952 and apply across all mines. They cover medical examination and certificate of fitness, welfare amenities (drinking water, sanitation, canteens, rest shelters, first aid and medical appliances), notice of accidents, and the constitution and functioning of safety committees and workmen's inspectors.
status_note: Continues in force under the OSH&WC Code transition (savings), pending replacement.

title: Mines Rescue Rules, 1985
summary: The Mines Rescue Rules, 1985 provide for the establishment and maintenance of rescue stations and rescue rooms, the training and qualification of rescue-trained persons, breathing-apparatus standards, and the organisation of rescue services required at mines liable to particular hazards. They govern the emergency-response capability that coal mines must keep ready.
status_note: Continues in force under the OSH&WC Code transition (savings), pending replacement.

title: Mines Vocational Training Rules, 1966
summary: The Mines Vocational Training Rules, 1966 require that persons employed in mines receive prescribed basic, refresher and skill training before and during employment. They set out the training infrastructure, syllabi and record-keeping obligations that mine managements must maintain for workforce competency and safety.
status_note: Continues in force under the OSH&WC Code transition (savings), pending replacement.

title: Coal Mines Regulations, 1957 (superseded)
summary: The Coal Mines Regulations, 1957 were the earlier principal safety code for coal mines under the Mines Act, 1952. They were superseded by the Coal Mines Regulations, 2017 and are retained for historical and reference purposes, including for interpreting provisions carried forward into the current code.
status_note: Superseded by the Coal Mines Regulations, 2017. Retained for reference.

title: Metalliferous Mines Regulations, 1961
summary: The Metalliferous Mines Regulations, 1961 (MMR 1961) are the DGMS safety code for non-coal (metalliferous) mines under the Mines Act, 1952. Although coal mines follow CMR 2017, MMR 1961 is a close analogue frequently referenced for competency, medical examination and safety provisions common to all mining.
status_note: Current safety code for metalliferous (non-coal) mines; analogue reference for coal.

title: The Code on Wages, 2019
summary: The Code on Wages, 2019 consolidates and replaces four earlier laws — the Payment of Wages Act, the Minimum Wages Act, the Payment of Bonus Act and the Equal Remuneration Act. It establishes a universal floor wage, statutory minimum wages, timely payment of wages and bonus, and equal pay, and applies to all establishments including coal mines. The four Labour Codes were brought into force on 21 November 2025.
status_note: In force from 21 Nov 2025.

title: The Industrial Relations Code, 2020
summary: The Industrial Relations Code, 2020 consolidates the Trade Unions Act, the Industrial Employment (Standing Orders) Act and the Industrial Disputes Act. It governs trade union recognition, standing orders, retrenchment and layoff, grievance redressal, strikes and lock-outs, and dispute resolution machinery. It applies to industrial establishments including mines and was brought into force on 21 November 2025.
status_note: In force from 21 Nov 2025.

title: The Occupational Safety, Health and Working Conditions Code, 2020
summary: The OSH&WC Code, 2020 consolidates thirteen labour laws on occupational safety, health and working conditions — including the Mines Act, 1952 and the Factories Act — into a single code. It covers working conditions, welfare facilities, hours of work, and safety obligations of employers, and provides the statutory basis (Section 136) under which the Draft OSH&WC (Coal Mines) Regulations, 2026 are being framed. It came into force on 21 November 2025.
status_note: In force from 21 Nov 2025; subsumes the Mines Act, 1952 (subordinate rules continue under savings).
source_filename: The Occupational Safety, Health and Working Conditions Code, 2020.pdf

title: The Code on Social Security, 2020
summary: The Code on Social Security, 2020 consolidates nine social-security laws covering provident fund, employees' state insurance, gratuity, maternity benefit, and the extension of social security to gig, platform and unorganised workers. It applies to establishments including coal mines and was brought into force on 21 November 2025.
status_note: In force from 21 Nov 2025.

title: Code on Wages (Central) Rules, 2026
summary: The Code on Wages (Central) Rules, 2026 are the final central rules operationalising the Code on Wages, 2019 for establishments under central-sphere jurisdiction. Published on 8 May 2026, they prescribe the manner of fixing minimum wages, floor wage, deductions, payment mechanisms and record-keeping.
status_note: Final central rules published 8 May 2026.

title: Industrial Relations Code (Central) Rules, 2026
summary: The Industrial Relations Code (Central) Rules, 2026 are the final central rules under the Industrial Relations Code, 2020, published on 8 May 2026. They detail procedures for trade union registration, standing orders, works committees, grievance redressal, and notices of change, layoff and retrenchment.
status_note: Final central rules published 8 May 2026.

title: Occupational Safety, Health and Working Conditions Code (Central) Rules, 2026
summary: The OSH&WC Code (Central) Rules, 2026 are the final central rules under the OSH&WC Code, 2020, published on 8 May 2026. They prescribe working-condition standards, welfare facilities, safety committees, health and working-hours requirements and duties of employers for central-sphere establishments.
status_note: Final central rules published 8 May 2026.
source_filename: Occupational Safety, Health and Working Conditions Code (Central) Rules, 2026.pdf

title: Social Security Code (Central) Rules, 2026
summary: The Social Security Code (Central) Rules, 2026 are the final central rules under the Code on Social Security, 2020, published on 8 May 2026. They set out the administration of provident fund, ESI, gratuity, maternity benefit and social-security coverage for gig, platform and unorganised workers.
status_note: Final central rules published 8 May 2026.

title: Model Standing Orders, 2026
summary: The Model Standing Orders, 2026 issued under the Industrial Relations Code, 2020 provide the default terms of employment — classification of workers, working hours, attendance, leave, conduct and discipline, and termination — applicable to industrial establishments, including mines, until certified standing orders are in place.

title: Mines and Minerals (Development and Regulation) Act, 1957
summary: The MMDR Act, 1957 is the central law regulating the development and regulation of mines and minerals other than petroleum and natural gas. It governs the grant of mineral concessions (prospecting licences, mining leases and mineral auctions), royalty and the District Mineral Foundation, and empowers rules on mineral conservation and development. This consolidated version incorporates amendments through 2023.

title: Mineral Conservation and Development Rules, 2017
summary: The Mineral Conservation and Development Rules, 2017 (MCDR 2017), notified as G.S.R. 144(E) under the MMDR Act, 1957, prescribe scientific and systematic mining practices, mining-plan and mine-closure-plan requirements, mineral conservation measures, and reporting to the Indian Bureau of Mines. They apply to minerals other than coal, atomic and minor minerals, but are a key governing reference in the mineral-regulation framework.

title: Minerals (Other than Atomic and Hydrocarbon Energy Minerals) Concession Rules, 2016
summary: The Mineral Concession Rules, 2016 (MCR 2016), made under the MMDR Act, 1957, govern the grant, renewal, transfer and management of mineral concessions such as mining leases and prospecting licence-cum-mining leases. This copy is amended up to January 2026.

title: Mineral (Auction) Rules, 2015
summary: The Mineral (Auction) Rules, 2015, made under the MMDR Act, 1957, set out the procedure for auctioning mineral blocks — the method of competitive bidding, eligibility, technical and financial conditions, and grant of mining leases through auction. This version is amended up to 29 January 2026.

title: Environmental Impact Assessment (EIA) Notification, 2006
summary: The EIA Notification, 2006 (S.O. 1533(E)), issued under the Environment (Protection) Act, 1986, makes prior environmental clearance mandatory for listed projects including coal mining. It sets out the screening, scoping, public consultation and appraisal process, categorises projects (A and B), and defines the role of the EAC/SEIAA. It is the core environmental-clearance regime that coal mine expansions and new projects must comply with.
authority: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change

title: Van (Sanrakshan Evam Samvardhan) Rules, 2023
summary: The Van (Sanrakshan Evam Samvardhan) Rules, 2023, in force from 1 December 2023, are made under the Van (Sanrakshan Evam Samvardhan) Adhiniyam (Forest (Conservation) Act, 1980, as amended). They prescribe the procedure for prior approval to divert forest land for non-forest use such as mining, including proposal processing, compensatory afforestation and conditions of approval. These Rules replaced the Forest (Conservation) Rules, 2022.
authority: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change
status_note: In force from 1 Dec 2023.

title: Forest (Conservation) Rules, 2022 (superseded)
summary: The Forest (Conservation) Rules, 2022 (G.S.R. 480(E)) prescribed the earlier procedure for approval of forest-land diversion for non-forest uses including mining. They have been superseded by the Van (Sanrakshan Evam Samvardhan) Rules, 2023, which took effect on 1 December 2023, and are retained for reference only.
authority: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change
status_note: Superseded by the Van (Sanrakshan Evam Samvardhan) Rules, 2023 (in force 1 Dec 2023).

title: EIA Notification 2006 — Consolidated Amendments Index
summary: A consolidated index of the amendments to the EIA Notification, 2006, listing the S.O. numbers and dates of the numerous amendments made since 2006. It is a navigational reference to track how the environmental-clearance regime has evolved alongside the principal notification.
authority: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change

title: Mining Plan and Mine Closure Plan Guidelines, 2025 (MPG-2025)
summary: The Mining Plan and Mine Closure Plan Guidelines, 2025 (MPG-2025), issued by the Ministry of Coal on 31 January 2025, consolidate the framework for preparing coal mining plans and progressive and final mine closure plans, including escrow-based closure-cost provisioning. They supersede earlier mining-plan guidelines and absorb the substance of the earlier closure-cost office memoranda.

title: Corrigendum to MPG-2025 (26 May 2025)
summary: A corrigendum/revision issued by the Ministry of Coal on 26 May 2025 to the Mining Plan and Mine Closure Plan Guidelines, 2025 (MPG-2025), correcting and clarifying provisions of the principal guidelines.

title: Mining Plan Guidelines, 2019 (closure-cost basis; superseded)
summary: The Ministry of Coal Mining Plan Guidelines dated 16 December 2019 established the mine-closure-cost rate basis (per-hectare provisioning, with a base date of 1 April 2019) underpinning escrow arrangements for coal mines. Though superseded by MPG-2025, they remain the key reference for the closure-cost rate basis.
status_note: Superseded by MPG-2025 (31 Jan 2025); retained for the closure-cost rate basis.

title: CEA (Safety Requirements for Construction, Operation and Maintenance of Electrical Plants and Electric Lines) Regulations, 2023
summary: The CEA Safety and Electric Supply Regulations, 2023, made by the Central Electricity Authority under the Electricity Act, 2003, prescribe electrical safety standards for the construction, operation and maintenance of electrical plants and lines. In coal mines they govern surface and underground electrical installations, working with CMR 2017's electricity provisions.

title: Central Electricity Authority (Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply) Regulations, 2010
summary: The CEA (Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply) Regulations, 2010 were the earlier statutory electrical-safety regulations under the Electricity Act, 2003, governing electrical installations including those in mines. They are the predecessor to the 2023 CEA safety regulations and remain a common reference for electrical safety compliance.
status_note: Superseded by the CEA Safety Regulations, 2023.

title: DGMS (Tech)(S&T) Circular 02 of 2025 — Ventilation (CMR Reg. 153)
summary: DGMS Technical (S&T) Circular 02 of 2025, dated 17 April 2025, prescribes the minimum quantity of air for continuous-miner and longwall districts in underground coal mines, interpreting Regulation 153 of the Coal Mines Regulations, 2017. It is a key working reference for mine ventilation planning and compliance.

title: DGMS (Tech) Circular 01 of 2025 — Quality and Condition of Explosive Materials
summary: DGMS Technical Circular 01 of 2025, dated 17 April 2025, addresses the quality and condition of explosive materials used in mines, prescribing precautions for their storage, handling and use to prevent misfires and premature blasts.

title: DGMS (Tech) Circular 05 of 2025 — Underground Coal and Metalliferous Mines Safety
summary: DGMS Technical Circular 05 of 2025, dated 8 October 2025, issues directions on safety in underground coal and metalliferous mines, reinforcing precautions for safe underground working.

title: DGMS (Tech) Circular 06 of 2025 — Prevention of Fall of Person from Height or into Depth
summary: DGMS Technical Circular 06 of 2025, dated 24 November 2025, prescribes precautions to prevent fall-of-person accidents from height or into depth in mines, including edge protection, fall-arrest arrangements and safe work practices.

title: DGMS (Tech) Circular 02 of 2024 — Precautions Against Danger of Inundation
summary: DGMS Technical Circular 02 of 2024, dated 22 May 2024, prescribes precautions against the danger of inundation (water inrush) in mines, including surveys of old and adjacent workings, barriers and advance boreholes. It supports the inundation-related provisions of the Coal Mines Regulations, 2017.

title: DGMS (Tech) Circular 03 of 2020 — Guideline for Scientific Study under Regulation 106
summary: DGMS Technical Circular 03 of 2020, dated 16 January 2020, lays down the guideline for conducting the scientific study of strata behaviour and support required under Regulation 106 of the Coal Mines Regulations, before adopting depillaring or mechanised extraction.

title: DGMS (Tech) Circular 10 of 2020 — Lightning Protection in Mines
summary: DGMS Technical Circular 10 of 2020 prescribes measures for lightning protection in mines, particularly to prevent lightning-induced initiation of explosives and hazards to surface installations.

title: DGMS (Tech) Circular 13 of 2020 — Medical Examination (Regulation 31)
summary: DGMS Technical Circular 13 of 2020 addresses the initial and periodical medical examination of persons employed in mines and the certificate of fitness, clarifying obligations under the medical-examination regulations. It is a common compliance reference for occupational-health requirements.

title: DGMS (Tech) Circular 14 of 2020 — Precautions Against Premature Blast of Site Mixed Emulsion
summary: DGMS Technical Circular 14 of 2020 prescribes precautions against premature blasting when using Site Mixed Emulsion (SME) explosives in opencast mines, covering charging, stemming and safe handling to prevent accidents.

title: DGMS Approval Circular 01 of 2020 — Standards for Man Riding Systems in Mines
summary: DGMS Approval Circular 01 of 2020 adopts standards for Man Riding Systems used to transport persons in mines (including underground man-riding installations), specifying design, safety and approval requirements.

title: DGMS (Tech) Circular 01 of 2018 — Diesel Equipment Standards
summary: DGMS Technical Circular 01 of 2018 lays down standards for diesel-operated equipment used in coal and metalliferous mines, covering permissibility, exhaust conditioning and safe use of diesel machinery underground and on surface.

title: DGMS (Tech) Circular 01 of 2021 — First-Aid Training and Certification Standards
summary: DGMS Technical Circular 01 of 2021, dated 6 August 2021, prescribes standards for the conduct of first-aid training and the issue of first-aid certificates for persons employed in mines, supporting statutory first-aid and emergency-preparedness requirements.

title: DGMS (Legis) Circular 2019 — Equal Employment Opportunity for Women in Mines
summary: The DGMS Legislative Circular of 2019 (revised) provides for equal employment opportunity for women in mines, including deployment of women in below-ground and opencast workings beyond earlier restricted hours, subject to conditions and safeguards.

title: Explosives Rules, 2008
summary: The Explosives Rules, 2008, made under the Explosives Act, 1884 and administered by the Petroleum and Explosives Safety Organisation (PESO), regulate the manufacture, possession, sale, transport and use of explosives — including those used in mine blasting. They complement the blasting provisions of the Coal Mines Regulations, 2017.
status_note: Current explosives rules under the Explosives Act, 1884 (PESO).
